Industrial · Chic · Authentic

THE
HUB

4 concepts  ·  1 vision  ·  High-output excellence

◈ Poblenou, Barcelona

The Hub was born from the conviction that efficiency is not the enemy of pleasure. Each concept is engineered to deliver a precise, fast, and authentic culinary experience — in spaces where the rawness of steel and polished concrete finds balance with the warmth of light wood and stabilised moss walls.

The Hub · 01 / 04

THE HUB
PIZZA

High-temperature stone oven · Speed of execution · Fire-crafted, every time.

The Concept

A wide range of fully customisable pizzas built around a unique rectangular format — 20 cm wide, with a length chosen by the customer: from 30 cm up to 1.5 metres. Share it, split it, make it yours.

The Differentiator

A flexible, shareable product unlike any round-pizza competitor. The format adapts to solo diners, couples, and large groups — maximising average ticket and table experience.

20 cm
Fixed width
30 cm → 1.5 m
Your chosen length
∞
Topping combinations

← All conceptsSmashJapasPoké

© 2025 The Hub. All rights reserved.

The Hub · 02 / 04

THE HUB
SMASH

Gourmet burgers · High-output production · The perfect crust, every single time.

The Concept

Organic beef burgers in a wide variety of combinations, complemented by fries, salads, and side dishes — positioned firmly in the higher-quality segment. Every patty smashed to order on a hot chrome griddle.

The Differentiator

100% organic beef meets the smash technique's signature crust — bridging fast-food speed with premium-restaurant quality at a sustainable price point.

Organic Beef
Traceable sourcing
Smash Crust
Chrome griddle · High heat
Sides & Salads
Full meal offer

The Hub · 03 / 04

THE HUB
JAPAS

Precision · Freshness · Raw Station — Sushi & Japanese tapas, elevated.

The Concept

A small-format Japanese culinary offering combining speed with culinary richness. Gyozas, baozi, dumplings, fried wontons, tuna and salmon tartares, soups, and wok-based dishes — all crafted at the Raw Station.

The Differentiator

Japanese tapas are inherently social and shareable — driving higher average spend per table while matching the pace of a high-output operation. Precision without slow-down.

Gyozas & Baozi
Steamed & pan-fried
Tartares & Raw
Tuna · Salmon
Soups & Wok
Hot station
Wontons
Crispy fried bites

The Hub · 04 / 04

THE HUB
POKÉ

Health · Freshness · Full personalisation — Cold Station, built your way.

The Concept

A highly customisable bowl experience where customers define every element — size, base, proteins, ingredients, sauces and toppings. Aligned with current trends in healthy, flexible eating.

The Differentiator

Full personalisation means every customer is the chef. The Cold Station model keeps ingredients fresh, fast to assemble, and suited to both the health-conscious lunch crowd and the quality-seeking dinner guest.

Location Map
Why Poblenou

Located in the heart of Barcelona's 22@ innovation district — the city's densest concentration of tech companies, startups and creative agencies.

Crossroads of a high-density office population (lunch) and an established premium residential community (dinner) — exactly the dual-shift model The Hub is built for.

Inside The Hub

LEARN
MORE

01 —

Location & Legal Strategy

Strategic choice · Poblenou, Barcelona

A neighbourhood blending tech-workers at lunch and high-end residents at dinner — engineered for two distinct clienteles and maximum seat utilisation across the full day.

The Hybrid Advantage

Unlike pure dark kitchens constrained by the 400m restriction (Colau/Collboni regulation), The Hub operates under a mixed restauration licence — enabling installation in dense urban zones where industrial kitchens are banned outright.

Legal Justification

The plan includes a fully compliant on-site consumption area (40+ seats) and a central bar. This hybridisation secures our position in the urban fabric and differentiates us from delivery-only competitors.

02 —

Layout & Operational Flow

The space is divided into high-performance zones, each engineered to eliminate cross-contamination and maximise throughput.

Zone 01
The Bar Magnet
Heart of the Hub

The circular central bar manages all beverages and guest reception, acts as a soft psychological barrier between production and the dining zone, and centralises billing for all 4 brands.

Zone 02
Receiving & Storage
Back of House

A single dedicated technical entrance for all suppliers, flowing directly into shared cold rooms. One entry point eliminates cross-traffic and enables strict traceability.

Zone 03
4 Production Cells
Independent Kitchens

Each concept occupies its own independent kitchen quadrant with dedicated air extraction. Zero cross-contamination by design.

Zone 04
Central Plonge
Centralised Dishwashing

Strategically positioned to collect crockery from both dining room and all production kitchens without disrupting service flow. 90-second rapid-cycle dishwasher.

03 —

Neurodesign & Well-being

Method · Marta Ribas · Cognitive Sciences

We apply cognitive science to improve both staff performance and guest experience. Every design decision — light, sound, spatial navigation — is evidence-based.

Cognitive Mapping

The central bar acts as an immediate landmark — guests understand at a glance where to order and where to sit. No confusion, no friction, faster table turns.

Acoustic Control

Perforated wood panels and living plant walls absorb kitchen machinery noise, guaranteeing a calm dining atmosphere despite high-output cooking operations running simultaneously.

ZoneLight LevelColour Temp.Purpose
Production Kitchens500–700 luxCool whiteSafety, alertness, precision
Dining Room2,700 KWarm whiteRelaxation, appetite stimulation
04 —

HACCP & Equipment

Digital HACCP Monitoring

IoT sensors on all shared refrigeration units provide continuous temperature logging and instant alerts. Compliance is automated — no manual checks, no gaps.

Allergen Zoning

A strictly separated Raw preparation zone (Japas / Poké) is physically isolated from all Gluten cooking zones (Pizza / Smash). Eliminated by architecture, not procedure alone.

Pizza
  • Compact high-efficiency rotary stone oven
  • High-temperature deck (450°C+)
  • Dedicated cold dough station
Smash
  • Hard chrome flat-top griddle (easy clean)
  • Fresh-air induction hoods
  • High-speed bun toaster station
Japas / Poké
  • Dedicated raw preparation station
  • Refrigerated sushi display counter
  • Cold assembly line (Poké)
Shared
  • Rapid-cycle dishwasher (90s cycle)
  • Modular cold rooms (IoT-monitored)
  • Central POS system (4 brands, 1 terminal)

Financial Model

BUDGET &
OPEX

QUADRANT BCN · Annual Operating Budget

Our OPEX is not a cost structure — it is a strategic statement. Every percentage point has been allocated with intent: to build a brand that operates with surgical precision, scales reliably, and dominates the Barcelona market long-term.

OPEX Breakdown
Personnel (Labor)41% · €656,000
Raw Materials (Food Cost)30% · €480,000
Delivery Commissions18.3% · €292,800
Rent & Utilities5.9% · €95,000
Marketing & Misc.2.8% · €45,000
TOTAL OPEX98% · €1,568,800
Cost Category% RevenueAnnual Amount
Raw Materials30%€480,000
Personnel (Labor)41%€656,000
Delivery Commissions18.3%€292,800
Rent & Energy5.9%€95,000
Marketing & Misc.2.8%€45,000
TOTAL OPEX98%€1,568,800
Strategic Analysis
41%
Labor Cost
The Performance Choice

This is our largest investment. While most competitors cut staff, we have intentionally doubled ours. A reinforced team means near-zero error rates, faster throughput, and a guest experience that justifies premium pricing across all four concepts.

30%
Food Cost
The Power of Synergy

By operating as a Hub, we centralise receiving for all four brands. This logistical synergy stabilises our Food Cost at 30% through bulk purchasing, while maintaining the surgical freshness required for Japas and Poké raw stations.

18.3%
Delivery Commissions
The Visibility Fee

With 61% of our business from delivery, we pay ~€292,000 in commissions. We view this as our digital rent. Because our staff is reinforced, our error rate is near zero — protecting our margin from costly refunds and customer complaints.

5.9%
Rent & Utilities
Industrial Efficiency

Operating a 350 m² facility in Poblenou is expensive — high-power air extraction alone is a significant cost. But by splitting these fixed costs across four concepts, we achieve an efficiency a traditional single-brand restaurant could never match.

2%
Strategic Conclusion

98% OPEX. 2% EBITDA.
A DELIBERATE SAFETY MARGIN.

This is not a low margin by accident — it is a protection strategy. We have chosen to invest in professional staffing and strict hygiene rather than risk a health crisis or operational failure. QUADRANT BCN is a scalable model built for reliability and long-term dominance in the Barcelona market.

€1.6M
Total OPEX
61%
Delivery revenue share
2%
EBITDA margin

Food Safety & Operations

HACCP &
FLOOR PLANS

QUADRANT BCN · Operational Standards

Food safety at The Hub is not a checklist — it is architecture. Every zone, every flow, every piece of equipment has been selected and positioned to enforce HACCP compliance by design, not by procedure alone.

Floor Plans
01 —

General Layout: The Hybrid Ecosystem

The QUADRANT BCN floor plan is divided into functional zones to ensure a seamless Forward Flow (Marche en Avant). Every square foot is optimised to maximise the 40% public-facing requirement while maintaining a high-performance industrial core.

HACCP Principle

Clear physical barrier between the "Dirty Zone" (loading dock, waste, raw storage) and the "Clean Zone" (preparation, assembly, dining). Raw ingredients only move forward — never back.

Click to enlarge
02 —

Guest Journey: Intuitive & Safe Circulation

Designed around Neuroarchitecture principles. A clear Cognitive Map guides customers to digital kiosks away from production noise. Guest flow is a one-way loop: Entrance → Digital Order → Dining → Pick-up.

HACCP Principle

Guests remain strictly within the front-of-house safety zone, never intersecting with internal staff paths or raw food handling areas.

Click to enlarge
03 —

Cooks' Pathway: Precision & Performance

Each kitchen functions as an independent high-performance quadrant. Staff enter via a dedicated technical access, moving directly to changing rooms before entering their production zone. Movement follows Work Triangle ergonomics.

HACCP Principle

Each cook operates in a dedicated environment to prevent allergen cross-contact. Forward Flow is strictly enforced: cold storage → preparation → pass. Dirty logistics never cross clean dishes.

Click to enlarge
04 —

Cashier & Runner Flow: The Operational Pivot

Positioned at the central Bar Magnet, staff bridge the four production quadrants and the customer. Their movement is optimised for speed and visibility, overseeing order status across all brands simultaneously.

HACCP Principle

Runners only handle final, packaged products. Their path avoids kitchen interiors entirely — ensuring total separation between the clean service flow and active production zones.

Click to enlarge
Equipment Standards
E.01 —

Cold Chain & Storage Equipment

Separated Cold Rooms

Segregated cold storage: organic meat (Smash) physically separated from raw fish (Japas / Poké), eliminating cross-contamination at source.

Blast Chillers

Mandatory for all cooked preparations. Required to bring items from 63°C to 10°C in under two hours — the critical HACCP cooling window.

Calibrated Probe Thermometers

Core temperature checks at goods reception and throughout storage. All thermometers are calibrated and logged for continuous cold chain traceability.

E.02 —

Preparation & Cooking Equipment

Pizza

High-temperature pizza oven with a minimum 1.5 m capacity. Professional dough mixer dedicated to the concept.

Smash

Professional plancha/grill with powerful extraction and refrigerated drawers under the work surface, keeping organic patties at safe temperature until cooking.

Japas

High-power wok burners, steam cookers for gyozas and baos, large-capacity rice cookers. All dedicated to the concept.

Poké

Refrigerated preparation tables (saladettes) maintaining all fresh ingredients and toppings between 0°C and 4°C — critical for raw fish safety.

E.03 —

Hygiene & Personnel Safety

Hands-Free Washbasins

Activated by pedal or photoelectric cell. Each station equipped with bactericidal soap dispenser and single-use paper towels.

Colour-Coded Cutting Boards

■ Red — Red meat   ■ Blue — Fish   ■ Green — Vegetables. Prevents bacterial transfer between the four concepts.

Stainless Steel Utensils

The only material authorised across all zones — near-zero porosity and ease of disinfection. All utensils are tracked and sanitised on a documented schedule.

E.04 —

Waste Management & Flow Control

Dedicated Dishwashing Room

Professional short-cycle dishwasher — final rinse at 85°C (HACCP disinfection threshold). Positioned to collect crockery from both dining room and kitchens without crossing clean areas.

Foot-Pedal Bins

Hermetically sealed, pedal-operated waste bins in every kitchen zone — eliminating hand contact with waste surfaces during service.

Grease Management System

Mandatory grease trap before all drainage — essential for Smash and Japas frying operations. Compliant with Barcelona municipal drainage regulations.

Legal & Compliance

LEGAL &
COMPLIANCE

QUADRANT BCN · Barcelona 22@ District

QUADRANT BCN is a hybrid gastronomic establishment operating under a "Restauració Mixta" licence — a model that distinguishes us from traditional industrial kitchens and anchors us in Barcelona's urban fabric with full regulatory compliance.

1. Business Identity & Licensing

QUADRANT BCN operates under a "Restauració Mixta" (Mixed Restoration) licence, in strict compliance with Barcelona's Urban Planning regulations (Pla d'Usos). We guarantee that a minimum of 40% of our total surface area is dedicated to public-facing use — including our guest dining area, the central bar, and the "Click & Collect" zone.

40%
Public-facing area
40+
Guest seats
4
Independent concepts

2. Food Safety & HACCP Standards

Our operational core implements a rigorous HACCP system across all four specialised kitchens.

Forward Flow Principle

Raw materials enter through a dedicated rear entrance and only move forward through storage, preparation, and cooking — never reversing into a clean zone.

Path Segregation

"Clean" circuits (food preparation and service) never intersect with "dirty" circuits (waste disposal and raw material reception).

Continuous Monitoring

All storage environments equipped with IoT sensors providing 24/7 temperature control and automated HACCP logging across the full cold chain.

3. Operational Ergonomics & Well-being

Optimised Pathing

Dedicated pathways for Cooks, Runners, and Guests ensure fluid movement and reduce physical strain across all operational zones during peak service.

Sanitary Boundaries

Staff pathways are strictly separated from public areas, maintaining the highest hygiene standards without compromising the guest experience.

4. Allergen Transparency — EU Regulation 1169/2011

In accordance with EU Regulation 1169/2011, QUADRANT BCN provides full transparency regarding the 14 major allergens across all four concepts. Digital allergen matrices are available at every ordering kiosk and below.

Note: while each quadrant operates in a physically separated environment, they share a central logistical core. Customers with severe allergies should inform staff.

5. Data Privacy & GDPR

Your privacy is paramount. Any data collected through our "Click & Collect" platform is processed in strict accordance with GDPR. We use your information solely to facilitate your orders and improve your experience. You hold the right to access, rectify, and request deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting contact@thehub.com.
